source.mcpMessage: Assets were manually exported by the user because Figma MCP hit the Starter-plan tool-call limit.

preferredRootScreenshot.use: Full-frame visual reference for rebuilding the Next.js portfolio slice and checking spacing, hierarchy, and responsive crop decisions.

manualSteps: Open the Figma URL and select node 379:12366.
Export the selected frame as PNG to public/figma-assets/work-copy-node-379-12366-full-frame.png.
For vector-only decorative layers, export SVG where available and preserve layer names in filenames.
For raster photos, avatars, thumbnails, or logos, export PNG at 2x unless the source asset is already a JPG/photo export.
Update dimensions in this manifest after placing files. Dimensions are currently null because MCP binary export was unavailable.

assets.nextUsage: Use only as a build reference or QA overlay, not as production UI.
Hero section avatar/profile visual. Use with next/image and meaningful alt text.
Brand mark in header or hero if present in the design. Inline SVG or serve as image depending on current code style.
Decorative hero background layer. Keep non-critical and hide from assistive tech.
Primary image for the first featured project card. Updated to match the latest user-exported asset dimensions.
Primary image for the second featured project card. Updated to match the latest user-exported asset dimensions.
Primary image for the third featured project card if present. Updated to match the latest user-exported asset dimensions.
Logo/icon for compact project listing. Prefer SVG for crisp scaling.
Logo/icon for compact project listing. Prefer SVG for crisp scaling.
Logo/icon for compact project listing. Prefer SVG for crisp scaling.
Company/client logo in experience section. Use accessible text nearby rather than relying on logo alt alone.
Company/client logo in experience section. Use accessible text nearby rather than relying on logo alt alone.

Blog or writing section visual if present in the design.

Updated Redooceit featured project card used by the Next.js slice.

notes: Assets were supplied manually by the user and wired into the Next.js component slice.
Full-frame screenshot is retained only as visual QA/reference and is not rendered as production UI.
Hero abstract background could not be exported separately, so the implementation uses a coded CSS approximation.
Featured project 03 is served from featured-project-03-v2.png to force the updated Redooceit visual and avoid stale Next image cache.
